description: The food.gdt dataset is loaded in Gretl, containing 'food_exp' and 'income' variables. Estimate the regression model 'food_exp = b0 + b1*income + error' using Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE). Create and save a Gretl script to /home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/mle_food.inp that initializes parameters b0, b1, and sigma (e.g., 80, 10, 50) and defines an 'mle' block using the normal log-likelihood: 'logl = -0.5*log(2*$pi) - log(sigma) - 0.5*((food_exp - b0 - b1*income)/sigma)^2'. After running the estimation, save the resulting coefficients for b0, b1, and sigma to /home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/mle_results.txt.
